dagSeveritySeparation.rule: P0-P3 values describe DAG or audit consequence only; localization quality records use critical, major, minor, or neutral.

qualitySeverities.description: Artifact is unsafe or unusable for the target context: broken rendering, protected content loss, meaning inversion, or release/export blocker without explicit waiver.
Material meaning, policy, term, tone, or locale defect that target-language players would notice and that should be repaired before a quality claim.
Low-impact defect that is noticeable but does not change core meaning and may ship only with reviewer rationale.
Traceable note, duplicate, false positive, reviewer question, or intentional accepted deviation that does not count as a quality defect.

categories.description: Target content does not preserve source meaning or intended player choice semantics.

subcategories.description: Target expresses the wrong meaning for the source.
Source content that should be present is missing from the target.
Target adds meaning not present in the source or context.
Target is more specific than the source or context supports.
Target is too vague and loses required source specificity.
A menu choice, branch option, or gameplay prompt changes the player's intended action.
Target meaning is wrong because scene, speaker, route, or runtime context was misread.

categories.description: Target content violates glossary, canon, term consistency, or romanization policy.

subcategories.description: Target uses an incorrect term for a known concept.
Target uses multiple terms where one term is required.
Target ignores a glossary or termbase rule.
Target changes a character, place, item, faction, skill, or lore name against canon policy.
Target uses the wrong romanization system or inconsistent romanized form.

categories.description: Target content violates style guidance, idiomaticity, readability, genre fit, or consistency expectations.

subcategories.description: Target conflicts with a cited style-guide rule.
Target is grammatical but unnatural for the locale.
Target style shifts without source, speaker, or scene reason.
Target is unnecessarily hard to read in context.
Target does not fit the game's genre, era, or narrative mode.

categories.description: Target gives a speaker, relationship, formality level, honorific, or emotional stance the wrong voice.

subcategories.description: Target is too formal or informal for policy and context.
Target breaks an established character voice.
Target misrepresents social distance, respect, intimacy, or hostility.
Target uses honorifics, titles, or address forms contrary to locale-branch policy.

categories.description: Target content violates locale formatting, cultural reference, shortcut, measurement, currency, or sensitivity requirements.

subcategories.description: Target uses the wrong date, time, or number format.
Target uses the wrong measurement, unit, or currency convention.
Target reference is confusing, inappropriate, or loses required cultural intent.
Target introduces avoidable offense or mishandles sensitivity policy.
Target shortcut, mnemonic, or input hint is wrong for the locale.

categories.description: Target corrupts, drops, translates, invents, or misorders protected spans.

subcategories.description: A required variable, placeholder, or interpolation is missing.
A placeholder or variable name was translated when it should be preserved or mapped.
Markup tags are missing, malformed, reordered incorrectly, or mistranslated.
Engine control codes, ruby tags, color tags, waits, or command spans are invalid.
A protected do-not-translate span was localized or altered.

categories.description: Target fails in its presentation slot, including overflow, truncation, wrapping, visibility, or script/font fit.

subcategories.description: Target exceeds available space or is cut off.
Line wrapping or manual breaks harm readability or meaning.
Target text is present but hidden, off-screen, or unreadable at runtime.
Ruby, furigana, or pronunciation guide rendering is wrong.
Localized image text, overlay text, or UI label placement does not match the asset.

categories.description: Patch, encoding, schema, asset binding, or runtime integration breaks the localized artifact independent of wording quality.

subcategories.description: Target characters are corrupted by encoding, normalization, or font support.
A bridge, patch, runtime, finding, or benchmark payload violates its schema.
Patch or delta application writes the wrong content or loses required content.
Runtime link, asset reference, label, route, or cross-reference is broken.
Localized text is attached to the wrong asset, speaker, event, or UI element.

rootCauses.description: Source text or asset is wrong, ambiguous, corrupt, or internally inconsistent.
Source annotations, protected-span metadata, speaker data, or context markings are missing or wrong.
Style guide is missing, contradictory, stale, or wrong for the target branch.
Glossary, canon-name, do-not-translate, or romanization policy is missing, contradictory, or stale.
Drafting or QA task assembled incomplete, stale, or misleading context.
Model output is wrong despite adequate context and policy.
Human reviewer, editor, or post-editor introduced or failed to catch the defect.
Rule produced a false positive, false negative, or encoded the wrong policy.
Patching, delta application, or verification changed or lost localized content.
Runtime evidence exposes engine, font, fixed-size UI, BiDi, input, or other i18n limits.
Defect was deliberately injected for seeded-defect evaluation.
Temporary value before human or oracle adjudication; draft/internal reports only.

seededDefectKinds.description: Inject a target phrase that changes the source proposition.
Remove required source content from the target.
Invert or redirect a player choice, route, or action prompt.
Use a term disallowed by glossary or canon policy.
Use multiple target terms for the same canon concept.
Change a character's established voice or relationship stance.
Use a target-locale formatting convention incorrectly.
Introduce or preserve a cultural reference that fails the target-locale policy.
Remove a required variable, placeholder, or interpolation.
Corrupt engine control codes, markup, or protected command spans.
Translate or alter a span explicitly marked for preservation.
Make target text exceed a known UI, textbox, or asset bound.
Place text off-screen, behind another element, or outside a visible runtime path.
Introduce invalid encoding, normalization, or unsupported rendered characters.
Make a bridge, patch, delta, runtime, finding, or benchmark payload invalid or non-applyable.

adjudicationStates.description: Finding exists but has not been reviewed; draft reports only.
Reviewer agrees this is a real defect and keeps or corrects taxonomy fields.
Reviewer finds no defect or no supported evidence.
Finding repeats another active finding; metrics credit only the canonical one.
Reviewer cannot judge from available source, target, policy, or runtime evidence.
Issue is intentional, policy-approved, or explicitly accepted with rationale.
Defect was repaired and reviewer verified the new evidence.

metrics.description: Expected seeded defects detected at least once divided by expected seeded defects.
Seed-matched confirmed findings divided by all findings claiming a seed match.
Confirmed non-duplicate findings divided by adjudicated non-duplicate findings.
Share of confirmed findings whose primary category matches the reviewer or oracle category.
Share of confirmed findings whose qualitySeverity matches the reviewer or oracle severity.
Share of confirmed findings whose primary root cause matches the reviewer or oracle cause.
Critical defects detected divided by adjudicated or seeded critical defects.
Findings missing required fields, evidence, taxonomy ids, or adjudication divided by all emitted findings.
